entry.title: SAP Stammtisch Melbourne Mar '24
entry.content:
Time to raise a toast to the SAP Dev Community in Melbourne. Let's take the opportunity to catch up as we get well into 2024. No agenda, no requirements, just come along and be involved in the way you would like.

To coincide with the SAP BTP Innovation Day in Melbourne on Thursday 21st March 2024 at 5:30pm at The Bank on Collins

 Ground floor/394 Collins St, Melbourne VIC 3000.

 

When you arrive, you can ask for a booking under "SAP Regulars Table" 😉

I am really looking forward to sharing this informal community get together. Pass the word around!

See you there.

Ben.

entry.title: Dynamic Date Filter Issue in a Story Based on Live Connection Model [SAC]
entry.content:
Hi all, we are trying to create a story using the ACTIVITY_LOG model which is a live model within SAC that is used to show info in the system overview.

Using this model as base, we created a story(2.0), in it we have a table, measures and dimensions from the model were selected and data is displayed, but when the date filter is set using "Date(Range)", no data is visible, were as when a date filter is set using "Date(Member)" data is visible, so is there any limitation in setting a range date filter when the story is based on a liver model. What we wanted was to set the filter for date dynamically to show the previous 30 days of data using the system date as the base.

Any help in this regard would be greatly appreciated.

entry.title: SAP BTP Partner Hands-on Excellence Program Workshop for Partners on SAP Datasphere.
entry.content:
A 2-day hands-on workshop to increase your knowledge and expertise with SAP Datasphere. 

We are planning for a hands-on workshop with SAP Datasphere and the idea is to help you get started with SAP Datasphere and how it can help enterprises to connect and collect data through data virtualization, replication, and orchestration from SAP and third-party sources across clouds and with on-premise applications.

In this workshop you can learn from product experts on how to leverage SAP Datasphere and unify your data and analytics in a multi-cloud solution that provides data integration, database access, data modeling, business semantics, and analytics for a data-driven enterprise. Business users can explore data, integrate new information, and share insights within secure, governed virtual "spaces". With in-depth topic overviews and practical exercises, you will gain knowledge and valuable experience in this end-to-end app development.  In addition, you learn about opportunities to collaborate and to run joint business outcomes in dedicated info-sessions about SAPs Partner Business and SAP Discovery Center.

Expand your network and gather insights from your peers working in other companies.

Thanks

entry.title: Re: SAP DM: Use OEE Time Model Elements to calculate and...
entry.content: You should be able to easily interlink them using Production Processes. Br, Manoel

entry.title: Re: SAC passing parameter from input control with single selection
entry.content: Note - there is an influence request for this item here - please vote on it to register interest and to get notified when the feature is added to SAP Analytics Cloud

entry.title: SAP Review Booklet Designer – Enhancement to Reporting
entry.content:
Review Booklet Designer:

You might have heard about the latest enhancement that SAP is working on, that is extensibility tools which aid to enhance the presentation requirement of Reporting and in this process, they have introduced a new tool Review Booklet Designer.

The Review Booklet Designer is one of the key user extensibility tools. You create your booklet in the test system and trigger the transport to the production system. With the aid of this tool you can create new pages or even copy the exting pages and make changes as required.

A little info about Review Booklet:

Review Booklet App Helps to display, analyze and validate data. They present an aggregated view of business data by combining predefined business pages, which are usually grouped under main areas.

Review booklets represent the next generation of multidimensional analytical applications. They reuse analytical components, content, and features from SAP Analytics Cloud in UI5 and thus create UI consistency between SAP S/4HANA Cloud and SAP Analytics Cloud. In review booklets, Fiori apps can embed multidimensional insights for a specific area.

 

 

 

Key Features:

App provides the following key features.

  • Create and manage views.
  • Display business data.
  • Global filters and page filters
  • Business pages
  • Colums and rows
  • Side panel
  • Analyze data at various levels of details.
  • Export data to spreadsheets: You can export individual business pages to spreadsheets by choosing Export to Microsoft Excel from the Report Menu ().
  • Navigate to other apps.

 

Review Booklet Designer: How to Create a Review Booklet in the Review Booklet Designer

 

First open - Manage KPIs and Reports app , Navigate to Review Booklet and select the Booklet you want to copy and click on “Copy”. (Note – you can copy only those whose status is Configuration Final or Application Active. )

 

Give you Boolet a Name

 

 

Click on “Open Configuration”, at the topmost corner

 

 

Here, you can see a blank canvas screen, where you can create your pages and report.

 

 

Under General Tab, you can see the information of the Report.  

 

Navigate to Business Pages Tab , here you can decide how to keep the sequence of the report and whether you what the user to see the visibility of the page . For Visibility, you can just tick or untick the Visible column.

 

 

 

Simply Click on any row on Business page ,where you want to add any page group or page , then Go to “Add” and select the option.

 

Give the page a name , a business group and select the page layout as desired from the below options.

 

You can select from the below options, who you want to display your page. Select the Data Provider, the Columns and Rows.

 

 

 

 

In Next Tab, maintain the Variables,

 

When done click on “Save Final” to save the report.

 

 

 

 

 

Go to Application Tab to create a Fiori Tile , you can see how its going to be visible on Fiori Home page and for easy navigation of the Report. After filling all details , click on Save and Publish.

 

On Fiori Home page , in search bar type your booklet name and click on it

 

 

Fill the parameters and select the page you want to analyse

 

 

 

 

 

At the Right most side, you can select page filters and Filter your data

 

 

Further References


SAP Help

SAP Best Practice

 

This post intends to give a round up on SAP Review Booklet Designer and the basic elements.

Thank you for going through the post. Your feedback and comments will help me make it more relevant to the community.

 

Give it a Hands on and explore the capabilities of SAP.

That’s All Folks!

Have a Great Day 😊

entry.title: Activating duplicate check in convergent invoicing
entry.content:
Activating duplicate check in convergent invoicing

There can be instances where bits can be sent again with same information due to issues in source system/interface. When such bits are received they get billed and invoiced automatically based on the setup.

Revering such duplicate data i.e., invoice, billing document and excepting bits is cumbersome activity.

To avoid such scenarios, activating duplicate check will restrict processing of duplicate bits further i.e. billing and invoicing.

Duplicate check configuration

Step1 : Define schema for duplicate check 

FICAIMG > convergent invoicing  > Basic Functions > Billable items > billable item management >Define schema for duplicate check 

Define duplicate check schema, name.

In processing rule if 3 Except Duplicates(Status Raw Excepted)is selected then the duplicated bits will be processed under status Raw Excepted  with the exception reason selected below.

In function module we can add Z functionality , if any additional  conditions to be added.

Check period to be added to validate new bits with previously created bits by number of days

 

Step2: Add fields for duplicate check

Based on the fields added system identifies the duplicate using the combination

 

Step 3: Assign schema  for duplicate check

FICAIMG > convergent invoicing  > Basic Functions > Billable items > billable item management >Assign schema for duplicate check

In this assign the duplicate schema created to billable item class

entry.title: Re: SAC passing parameter from input control with single selection
entry.content: Note - there is an influence request for this item here - please vote on it to register interest and to get notified when the feature is added to SAP Analytics Cloud.

entry.title: Re: SQL query to calculate delivery dates for the Frame Agreements (Sale Blanket Agreement)
entry.content: Your query contains StartDate and EndDate. Which of these dates is the "delivery date"? Could you please post a screenshot of the query result, and a table of with the expected result?

entry.title: Re: Library Copy Feature
entry.content:
Hi SravanthiVedula

Not aware of any feature which allowed a library to be copied within LMS. This just didn't exist.

You could always raise this an enhancement request in the Customer community portal and with business reasons, our Engineering team would look into this request based on the Kudos that it garners along with the reason of the request and they might implement. This usually takes time. Check KBA 2090228 that explains this.
 
An option which you could consider would be to firstly create a new library. Then use the Item Connector to associate any number of Items to the library you have just created.

If this information helped you then please consider giving Kudos or accepting this post as solution.

Padraig

entry.content:

Out of two, the builds go through for one of the apps sometimes, but for the other it always fails. Is this something you guys are working on?

 

SAP Build Apps


@EsmeeX @MaiaraEllwanger @liardo

This is regarding SAP Analytics cloud excel Addin.

Please note i'm able to change and process the existing data but facing the issue for any new data.

Thanks,

Lakshmi

entry.title: Automatic packing of EWM Outbound delivery based on Packing Instructions (UPB)
entry.content:
Automatic packing in EWM Outbound delivery was always required and so from version 2022, SAP came up with concept of UPB where in it was made possible to pack the outbound deliveries automatically by using Packing Instructions/Specification within the transaction.

As per the existing design the packing in outbound delivery is done manually, in which the user will select the picked item that needs to be packed and create a handling unit to pack the subsequent item. Drag and drop of product to pack it in the created handling unit is also available.

However, it is a tedious job to select every product/item manually and create HU’s to pack them.

To make this process easier for the customers, auto pack functionality is introduced- where the customer can simply choose the product to be packed and click on “Auto pack” button which will identify the appropriate packaging materials of the products and create the handling units automatically.

This article covers for Auto packing based on Packing Instructions instead of pack specification In EWM Outbound Delivery by using Unified Packaging Builder (UPB)

  • Config for UPB 
  • Master data
    • Packing Instructions (POP1) and Packing condition records (POF1)
  • Business Process 
    • Auto Pack based packing instructions for specific product and ship to party/Customer in EWM Outbound delivery

1. Config part

Logistics General => Handling Unit Management =>Automatic Packing => Unified Package Building

Activate UPB

Define Unified Package Building Profile -> Choose Packing Instructions

Maintain Packing Process Profiles

Define Procedure for Packing Instructions Determination

  • Add Condition Type 0OBD 

SPRO -> EWM -> Goods Issue -> Define Document Types for Outbound Delivery Process  

  • Assign UPB profile to Outbound document type  

 

2. Master Data -> For  Auto Packing (Nested) during Outbound after picking 

POP1 – Packing Instructions

Level 1 :

Level 2 :

Packing Instruction -> Warehouse tab ->  Maintain HU type in both the levels.

Packing Conditions records (POF1) ->Customer and Product specific Auto packing.

Enter Determination type -> 0OBD

Key combination -> Choose Material and Ship -to-party

Assign Packing Instructions for Customer and Product Master

3. Business Process : Auto Packing using of packing instruction per Customer and Product Master in EWM Outbound delivery

Pre-requisites are 

  • Picking -> Stock should move to Pack Workstation  or Good issue Zone.
  • Picking Status should confirmed status.

Go to Follow On functions -> Pack

Select  HU 

Control Tab -> Enter BIN

Click Button  -> Pack Automatically Button

System has packed based on packing instructions 23

Conclusion : EWM Outbound delivery is packed automatically based on Customer and Product Master by using packing Instructions (Unified Package Builder)

SAP Note 3301323 - Automatic packing for outbound delivery  -> implemented on  S4HANA 2022 (107)

entry.title: E4H Conversion Error in Phase MAIN_NEWBAS/XPRAS_AIMMRG
entry.content:
If you are trying to do a E4H Conversion from SAP GTS 11.0 to SAP GTS Edition for HANA 2023 with S/4 HANA Foundation 2022 then you may come across below error.

Issue:

During the execution phase below error is reported

Sql command that runs 

INSERT INTO "/SAPSLL/TEDTM" ( "MANDT", "STCCS", "RTYPE", "TLC_AART" ) SELECT DISTINCT

 "/SAPSLL/LC_CUSM"."MANDT", "/SAPSLL/TLEGSU"."CTSIM" as "STCCS", "/SAPSLL/LC_CUSM"."RTYPE",

 "/SAPSLL/LC_CUSM"."TLC_AART" FROM "/SAPSLL/LC_CUSM" INNER JOIN "/SAPSLL/TLEGSU" ON

 "/SAPSLL/LC_CUSM"."MANDT" = "/SAPSLL/TLEGSU"."MANDT" AND "/SAPSLL/LC_CUSM"."LGREG" =

 "/SAPSLL/TLEGSU"."LGREG" WHERE "/SAPSLL/TLEGSU"."LGREG" = "/SAPSLL/LC_CUSM"."LGREG" AND

 "/SAPSLL/TLEGSU"."SRVLL" = 'LECCUS' AND "/SAPSLL/TLEGSU"."CTSTY" = '101' AND NOT (

 "/SAPSLL/LC_CUSM"."MANDT" = '000' )

Solution:

Old table: /SAPSLL/LC_CUSM

New table after conversion: /SAPSLL/TEDTM

The reason for the above error is that there are some duplicate entries that exists in /SAPSLL/TEDTM table so the XPRA report is not able to insert the content into the table and it throws an error.

While you can find similar kind of 301 error for different type of schema and table but there is no specific steps for this table which comes during the conversion.

Option1:

     Try to login to the clients and see if you can get help from functional consultant to identify the duplicate entries from the table and repeat the phase. (Take a backup before you do this)

Option 2: 

Accept the error at this stage and complete the conversion.

Once the conversion is done do below steps.

Check if the index exists.Most likely it will because its a ~0 index and its reserved index which means you can only see that in se14.It wnt even show in hana studio.

- Drop the index from below command from hana studio

ALTER TABLE "SAPABAP1"."/SAPSLL/TEDTM" DROP CONSTRAINT "/SAPSLL/TEDTM~0";

- For HANA databases, use the HANA scripts HANA_Data_DuplicateKeys_CommandGenerator_1.00.100+ and HANA_Data_DuplicateKeys_columnStore_Deletion_CommandGenerator attached in SAP Note 1969700 to remove duplicates

below the command that i used

**************************************

DELETE FROM
"SAPABAP1"."/SAPSLL/TEDTM"
WHERE
"$rowid$" IN
( SELECT
ROW_ID
FROM
( SELECT
"$rowid$" ROW_ID,
ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY "MANDT", "STCCS", "RTYPE") DUP_NO
FROM
"SAPABAP1"."/SAPSLL/TEDTM"
)
WHERE
DUP_NO > 1
)
WITH HINT (NO_AGGR_SIMPLIFICATION, NO_GROUPING_SIMPLIFICATION)

*******************************

@note: Please be double sure when you run this command.Have it verified with the functional consultant as its going to delete duplicate entries from the table.

- Once duplicates are removed, try to create the index again

ALTER TABLE "SAPABAP1"."/SAPSLL/TEDTM" ADD CONSTRAINT "/SAPSLL/TEDTM~0" PRIMARY KEY ( "MANDT", "STCCS", "RTYPE" )

- Run the XPRA again /SAPSLL/XPRA_V8000 (this is the same XPRA which ran during the execution phase)

and this time it should typically work without giving issues.

 

Hope this help.

Thanks

Sid

entry.title: Comparing Workflow Templates in SAP
entry.content:
Introduction

Identifying the changes present between different versions of workflow templates can be a challenge. Unlike other objects, there isn't a built-in functionality for direct comparison. Manually scrutinizing each step of the templates side-by-side can be both tedious and error-prone.

This lack can be frustrating, as I’ve witnessed firsthand through inquiries from few of my colleagues struggling to identify changes between versions for troubleshooting or maintaining consistency across environments. Fortunately, I’ve been able to help them by employing a couple of effective techniques that help bridging this gap.

While there are few solutions available online, I haven’t encountered a comprehensive resource specifically dedicated to guiding users through this process. Therefore, I’m writing this post with the hope of empowering you with effective method for comparing workflow template versions in SAP. Additionally, it serves as a future resource for my colleagues whenever they encounter similar challenges, allowing me to simply direct them to this guide.

Comparing Workflow Templates

Gearing Up:

  • For this post, I’ll be using two workflow templates which are nearly identical, except for a few differences which I have deliberately introduced in order to demonstrate the process.
  • We are going to need a text editor which supports two key functionalities: “Find” feature using regular expressions, as well as has feature to compare two text files (diff tool). I will be using Visual Studio Code for this as it offers both features seamlessly.
  • We will be comparing the two workflow templates by exporting the templates as XML file and comparing the exported files in Visual Studio code. However, there's a catch. By default, these exported files may be compressed and lack proper formatting, making it difficult to see any changes. To address this, we'll need to format the XML files using the "XML Tools" extension within VS Code. This extension transforms the raw XML into a human-readable format, allowing us to easily identify any modifications/differences when comparing the files using the built-in diff tool.

Now that we’ve got our list of tools prepared, let's explore the steps to identify differences in two workflow templates.

Step 1. Export the templates as XML file.

In this step, we will open both templates in Workflow Builder (SWDD) and export the templates as XML file. The menu option to export the files can be found at Menu > Workflow > Import/Export > Export to XML File > Export Workflow as Internal XML

Step 2. Open exported files in Visual Studio Code

In this Step, we will move the two exported files to a separate folder named “Compare” and open this folder in Visual Studio Code. To open the folder in Visual Studio Code, File > Open Folder command can be used from the menu bar.

Step 3. Formatting the XML files

It can be seen that the exported XML files are compressed and do not contain proper indentation. We will need “XML tool” extension to convert these XML files to readable format so that the changes are easily visible when compared.

The “XML tools” extension can be installed in Visual Studio code by following these simple steps:

  • Navigate to View menu.
  • Select Extensions
  • Search for “XML Tools” in the search bar
  • Click the install button.

 

Once the “XML Tools” extension is installed, go back to the XML files, right click on the XML code, and choose Format Document With > XML Tools. Do this for both XML files.

 

After formatting, the files will be transformed into a more readable format, similar to the files in following screenshot.

 

Step 4. Comparing the two files

In this step we will finally compare the differences between two XML files. To compare two files, right click on first file and chose “Select for Compare”, then right click on the second file and choose “Compare with Selected”.

 

The difference checker tool should now open in the main panel of Visual Studio code, and the differences between two files should be highlighted in Red and Green. An overview of all changes present throughout the whole file can also be seen at the right-hand side present in a panel called mini-map (highlighted in below screenshot). This mini-map offers a quick glimpse at all modifications throughout the files and facilitates scrolling when clicked on the highlighted part on minimap.

 

As can be seen in the mini-map every difference present in two XML files is highlighted. However, the changes including workflow IDs, version numbers, and timestamps have also been highlighted. These differences aren't relevant to our goal. These irrelevant differences are creating noise in the difference checker tool, making it harder to identify the actual changes we're interested in. To address this, we'll strategically remove these irrelevant differences from both files, ensuring only the significant modifications remain highlighted.

To eliminate these irrelevant differences, we will leverage the Regular expressions powered “find” feature of Visual studio code.

Let's begin by addressing the discrepancies caused by different workflow IDs within the two XML files. Press Ctrl+F to open find feature, enter the workflow ID in the find box and replace all occurrences of workflow ID with an empty string. Do this for both files.

 

In my case, removing the workflow IDs significantly reduced irrelevant highlights, however, the differences in "Changed Date" and "Changed Time" were still creating noise (highlighted below). To achieve a cleaner comparison, let's eliminate these discrepancies as well.

 

Removing individual <CHANGED_ON>,  <CHANGED_AT> and similar tags would not be efficient due to their multiple occurrences with  different enclosed timestamp within each XML file. Simple find and replace operation would not help because the timestamp enclosed within tags are different in same XML file. Instead, we'll utilize a regular expression to target all such tags effectively.

Here’s how to proceed:

  • In the Find input box, enter the following regular expression: <(CHANGED|ACTIVAT)_(ON|BY|AT)>[^<>]+
  • Enable the “Use Regular Expression” option (highlighted in screenshot below)
  • Replace all matched occurrences with and empty string in both XML files.

 

The above regular expression effectively targets all occurrences of Changed/Activated on/at/by tags and replaces those with empty string.

While much of the noise was removed after performing above step, examining the mini-map revealed additional noise in the form of <VCYPOS>, <VCXPOS>, <LCXPOS>, and <LCYPOS> tags (can be seen in below screenshot). My understanding of these tags is that they represent the X and Y coordinates of object/node positions within the workflow. As we're not interested in these changes, we can eliminate them from both XML files as well.

To achieve this, we will use <(LC|VC)(X|Y)POS>\d+ Regular expression. Enter this regular expression in the “find” box and replace all matched occurrences with empty string in both XML files.

 

 

Finally, with all the irrelevant differences eliminated, the comparison tool now displays only the four key changes within the XML files that we were originally looking for.

 

With the help of highlighted differences, I found the following changes that were made in workflow template:

  1. The template name was changed from ZTICKET1 to ZTICKET2 (Line 6).
  2. The description of the template was changed from “Example workflow for Ticket system (V1)” to “Example workflow for Ticket system (V2)”. This can be noted in line 7.
  3. The Object ID, similar to the workflow template ID, differs between the files. This is expected and is irrelevant to our analysis. We could've removed it during the de-noising process if it had numerous occurrences.
  4. Further down the file (line 1118, as indicated by the mini-map), I encounter another important change.
    This modification suggests that the Expression binding of node 50 has been changed from &_WF_INITIATOR& to &AGENT& which is indeed correct and can be confirmed from below image

 

Conclusion:

By following these steps, I was able to identify the exact changes that were made in the workflow template. This approach streamlined the process and ensured accurate identification of modifications made between different versions/templates of workflow.

I hope this guide has proven informative. Feel free to leave comments below with any questions or suggestions for further improvement.

Thank you for your time!

Regards

Dharshan

entry.title: Using an OData API get records back to back in a single page by clicking a button in SAP Build Apps.
entry.content:
Hi All,

I am using an OData API which is carrying around 1000 of records, we are calling first 20 records through pagination in data variable, is there any way to call the rest of the data in a form of 20 records in a repetitive manner on the same page by configuring a button in SAP Build Apps.

Thanks in advance,
Yashmin

@Dan_Wroblewski , @marihirvi , @StephanKoenig , @Ian_Thain , @SandipAgarwalla

entry.title: Interview Scheduling - Server Connection Failed: Exception fetching availability
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I'm seeking your advice regarding the Interview Scheduling feature in the SuccessFactors Recruiting module. In the screenshot below, the "Find Availability" button appears to be malfunctioning, displaying an error message stating "exception fetching availability." Although I can manually select custom slots, I encounter another error when proceeding to the next page to finalize scheduling, which states "exception occurred while saving interview details."

The Outlook and Microsoft Teams integration has not been enabled; the functionalities mentioned are solely within the Recruiting module. Has anyone else experienced similar issues? Your insights would be greatly appreciated.

entry.title: Re: Learn How to Create an Incentive Outcome Dashboard in Report Stories
entry.content:
Hello again!
Following some conversations with our customers there was been a requirement where access to Compensation Executive Review, worksheets, etc be made available from directly within the report story. This removes the need of having multiple tabs open as well as you being able to filter and visualise the report data, while also being able to access worksheets or Executive Review.
So, through using the suite deep links we can achieve this through adding a site from within the report. Below is an example:

report sample

To have this added into your report.

Edit the report> Insert> Add> Others> Web Page

Edit the tile which is added to the report and provide a header / title as well as the deep link. For all deep links available in SuccessFactors, click here. 

Hope this helps 🙂

Thanks!

entry.title: Re: how to compare Calculation view field structure betwe...
entry.content:
Hi Naiduz,

 

I don't believe such a mechanism exists for comparing different systems.
https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_HANA_PLATFORM/fc5ace7a367c434190a8047881f92ed8/b06adc3dec9a4cbfb4c232d7ff6c4353.html

You can compare old and new versions of the same view as described in this community topic :

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-q-a/how-to-perform-version-comparison-for-hana-calculation-views/qaq-p/622974

 

Kind Regards

Simon

entry.title: Re: Deep Dive: Footprint Calculation in SAP Sustainability Footprint Management
entry.content:
hi Nico, can I ask a strange question and this is because I've missed last couple of webinars. Is the understanding correct that SAP ESG suite of products will be offered only with RISE contract from March/April 2024 onwards? i.e.; On-prem customers won't be able to connect ESG suite(hosted on BTP)? Some of my colleagues have heard this from unknown source, which is difficult to believe. 

Kr, Debashish.

Thanks

Regards

Tanisha

entry.title: Error message F5A 192 when post document for TH
entry.content:
Dear Community,

This is for TH Billing when trying to post a billing document, getting error: Enter a Business Location. I have referred to Note 376018. The code corrections exists. However, this check is causing the error.

ACCIT_FI-BUPLA - Initial(Blank) as TH does not use Business Places in this case.

Could you please suggest?

Thanks!

entry.title: Re: Is direct SAP HANA database connectivity permitted in...
entry.content:
Hello Vijay,

 

Typical ODBC driver support for S/4 HANA Private Cloud with RISE is supported according to the services guide :
https://assets.cdn.sap.com/agreements/product-policy/hec/roles-responsibilities/rise-with-sap-s4hana-cloud-private-edition-and-sap-erp-tailored-option-english-v3-2022.pdf

Here is the ODBC connection guide :
https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_HANA_PLATFORM/0eec0d68141541d1b07893a39944924e/66a4169b84b2466892e1af9781049836.html

 

I know that S/4 Private HANA Cloud customers have somewhat more access to their HANA DB than S/4 HANA public customers and HANA Cloud etc, but the HANA Enterprise Cloud operations support team(XX-HST-OPR component) would be able to assist where needed for the process if required.

Kind Regards

Simon

entry.title: Re: How do I get columns with no Data to show up in a cro...
entry.content:
I can't believe nobody answered this question at the time. I was looking for just this: "How do I get columns with no data to show up in a crosstab". This must be an issue which lots of people have come across, surely. For example, you might want to show all the products you sell to show in a crosstab, regardless of whether you sold any during the week for which you ran your report. Webi normally doesn't work like this, and it only shows fields with data.

For me being able to do this was particularly important because the data is feeding into a bigger report in Excel, and missing columns cause annoying problems. I found a suggestion elsewhere in the SAP community, but I can't find it again now. I couldn't believe I'd not thought about this, but all it needed was a list of all columns (e.g. a list of all products you sell) in an Excel workbook, and using this as an additional data source. I then merged the dimensions and this solved the issue. 

Just adding this now in case it's of use for anyone else looking for a solution.

entry.title: Re: Using an OData API get records back to back in a sing...
entry.content:
Hi

yes possible. Use Previous, Next button to modify the $top & $skil parameters and call the API accordingly.

entry.title: Re: Create a oData server and add multiple entity
entry.content:
To create a single OData service with all four tables from the SNWD_EPM model, you can utilize associations to link the tables within the service. However, if there's no direct relationship between Set A (SNWD_PD and SNWD_BPA) and Set B (SNWD_SO and SNWD_SO_I), you may need to establish a connection between them through a common field or intermediary table.

Click here to explore various methods for establishing relationships between disparate sets of tables in your OData service.

entry.title: Can I include snapshot key figures in my planning are CPI-DS staging table for upload?
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I am trying to merge a currency based planning objects using CPI-DS.  The current planning level is Location/Customer/Product/Currency and I currently have multiple currencies per Location/Customer/Products.  I wish to combine these into a single record (the currency may vary between different Location/Customer/Products but there should only be one per combination). 

I am creating a dataflow to pull all the data and load all the converted values to my primary currency planning object and set all the alternate currencies to zero to allow a future deletion.  My issue is that the planning object has some snapshot key figures defined.  I am able to pull the data into CPI-DS for the snapshot figure, but after I am unable to get the snapshot key figures to show in my key figure staging table for exporting back into IBP.  I tried to turn on ENABLE_SNAPSHOT_KFS to see if that exposed the snapshot key figures but it was not a success (I assume this just allows me to manually upload via the Data Integration fiori app).

The question is whether it is possible to output to snapshot key figures directly out of a CPI-DS data flow and if so how,

Thanks,

Jez

entry.title: Re: Multiple billing document creation for zero quantity and Status completion is not feasible in VF
entry.content: From your post, it is not clear what is the issue. You may have to add more information on what is the issue with the related screen shots.

entry.title: Re: 🇩🇪 Hands-on with the btp CLI and APIs (Frankenthal, DE)
entry.content:
Hi @Kaustavsap2 ,

This is not an ASUG event but an DSAG event (German-speaking SAP user group). To see the event, please log in via https://dsagnet.de/login/ and then join the group AG BTP Development & Operations.

Afterwards, you should be able to use the link https://dsagnet.de/event/btp-betriebstag-1 (for this session) and also https://dsagnet.de/event/sap-code-jam-service-integration-with-sap-cloud-application-programming-model-1 (CodeJam on March 21th - more details here).

If you only want to join the CodeJam and not the BTP Operations Day (in German), I would recommend to join the CodeJam on March 21th.

entry.title: Prototype: Keep Track of those BTP Credits
entry.content:
Ever wondered where your CPEA Credits went? Or where they will be spent on in the near future? Which project is consuming what and which sub account is silently munching those credits away?

An often-ignored, yet very interesting service is the SAP Usage Data Management Service for SAP BTP which can provide you with all this insight. It tells you which service is used in which sub account, what its consumption is and how many credits you are paying for it. A wealth of information that, once neatly organized, can help you in monitoring your global account better, keeping track of your spending and identifying areas where money is spent with no real business return. And who doesn't like cost savings :-)? Moreover, coupled with a forecasting and alerting mechanism, it could identify anomalies and spikes in consumption which you rather deal with instantly instead of keeping that as a surprise for the monthly bill!

Ideations about this scenario gave rise to a prototype where we clubbed together a few BTP services:

  • Usage Data Management service: for all the great insights mentioned above;
  • Alert Notification service: to inform and alert users about spikes and anomalies;
  • Job Scheduling service: to regularly fetch new data points;
  • SAP Build Work Zone: to create and present a nice user interface on top of it;
  • Cloud Application Programming model: as the brains and glue for all the above.

Baptized with the name "BTP Resource Consumption monitor", the prototype is ready for testing and end-user feedback. It is made available as open source, and as such does not come with SAP Support, yet any feedback to improve the tool is most welcome.

 

Example of the consumption dashboard per service.

 

 

Ready to try it out for your own CPEA account? You can find the project files and installation documentation in the following SAP-Samples repository: https://github.com/SAP-samples/btp-resource-consumption-monitor

entry.title: Re: Material XXXX status obsolete does not allow the plan...
entry.content:
Hi,

Please check on the functionality on cross plant (Basic data view)/ plant specific material status (MRP1 view). Based on the business requirement, material can be restricted for some functions for an example, if the  material to be discontinued. Check configuration OMS4 'define material statuses' settings under material requirement. With the respective error messages, users preforming the transactions will be notified on the current status of the material.

entry.title: Re: I dream with a "Parameterized" Task Chain
entry.content: yes @MKreitlein, I have already registered a few 🙂 But I need a short term solution, for something that from my point of view is basic in an ETL. Right now I have projects with a very important overload of objects due to the lack of this functionality, and the maintenance is going to be a headache. That's why I have some urgency. Regards,

entry.title: Announcing the Water Track at the SAP for Energy and Utilities Conference 2024
entry.content:
Following the positive feedback received from last year's Water Track at the 2023 Utilities Conference in Basel, where passionate speakers showcased examples on how data can transform the water sector by tackling challenges such as contamination, leakages detection or the management of limited resources, we are proud to keep the track in the agenda for this year with the aim to consolidate this section for future conference editions and give floor to a selection of exceptional professionals working hard for a world in which water resources are managed in an equitable and sustainable way.

The soul of this track is the inheritance of the Executive Value Network for Water and Waste Water Companies meetings, this is a regular meeting of water companies which takes place in Europe (already five editions) and Latin America (third edition scheduled for May) where participants highlighted common  challenges water utilities are facing—water scarcity, augmenting capital expenditures, legislation, aging workforce, inflation in energy prices, and sustainability—along with case studies of how SAP customers are leveraging SAP solutions to address these challenges.

Executive Value Network participants in 2023 meeting at Cartagena de Indias, Colombia, and registration page for the 2024 meeting at Monterrey, Mexico.

Based on this feedback, and conducted by our colleague Ivan Barreto who is the Head of the Industry Advisory Utilities & Public Services for Latin America and ex-employee of Acueducto de Bogotá which serves water and waste water services in the capital of Colombia, the water track will take place on Wednesday, 24th April. It will provide insights into the ways SAP solutions can help organizations confront these challenges: Look forward to significant showcases, a product keynote and a discussion round table among participants which will provide a complete overview on how SAP can can help water companies gain operational efficiencies.  

Ivan Barreto conducting a session of the Exectutive Value Network for Water and Waste Water

The Agenda will be focused on cases where SAP solutions can help organizations confront these challenges and its operational benefits, starting with a short a keynote describing the concept architecture by SAP to support water management processes. It serves as a “framework”  highlighting ways in which organizations can leverage their software investments to tackle processes such as smart meter data management (“Smart Platform for Water: Vision, Feasibility and Benefits on Daily Operations”). It will be followed by transformation case studies:   

 

  • The Power of Smart Metering - Innovations in Alert Handling through Cutting-Edge Technologies and Mass Data Analysis,  by Inge Opreel FARYS CIO and responsible of the Smart Water Platform reference project.  
    • Brief overview of smart metering and the importance of alert handling
    • Current challenges and the impact on efficiency and reliability
    • Architecture, innovations and real-time analytics for proactive problem-solving
    • Use cases
    • Utilizing big data analytics (trend identification)

 

  • Leveraging SAP Service Cloud for Enhanced Customer Management: Lessons from Pidpa's Transformation Journey by Anke Geudens, Business Process Manager at Pidpa
    • Business Transformation: The strategic context behind Pipda's decision to adopt SAP Service Cloud and the impact on their overall business goals.
    • Customer Processes Involved: A deep dive into the specific customer-centric processes that were streamlined and enhanced with SAP Service Cloud.
    • Challenges and Opportunities: The key challenges faced during the implementation process and the opportunities that emerged as a result of adopting SAP Service Cloud.
    • Change and Learning: Strategies employed to manage change effectively throughout the organization, leveraging e-learning and in-app help mechanisms.
    • Business Outcomes and Future Plans: The tangible business outcomes achieved through SAP Service Cloud implementation and the future plans for further innovation and growth.

 

  • Aquafin’s Digital Transformation of Field Operations with SAP FSM by Sharda De Caluwé, Program Manager at  Aquafin and Laura Debrock, Senior Manager & Board Member at Delaware
    • Spotlight on Aquafin's operational planning division
    • Identifying the imperative for digitalization and selection tools
    • Navigating the implementation process
    • Exploring pitfalls and successes

 

  • Delivering Best in Class Customer Experience at Canal  de Isabel II by María González Cano, Comercial Direction Staff at Canal de Isabel II
    • This project represents the largest change management initiative in the company's 170-year history. It promises to modernize Canal de Isabel II and equip it to excel in the years to come.:
    • Vision: what we are looking for
    • Background: what we have had to struggle with
    • Solution: what we are working with
    • Our decision: SAP

 

  • SWDE’s Customer Field Services Management with SAP FSM – Benefits and Observations by Henri Pecheur, Director of Maintenance Optimisation at Société Wallonne Des Eaux -  SWDE
    • Setting the context
    • Objectives that drove the project's success
    • Results, metrics and achievements
    • Lessons learned: navigating challenges and solutions
    • The road ahead including future prospects and innovations

 

Take this unique opportunity to connect with your peers, gain insights into industry trends and chart your development plan with the latest technologies of 2024!

 Looking forward to seeing you in Seville!

entry.title: InvalidTaxException : Bad tax framework feature in SAP CC
entry.content:
Hi Experts, 

We are getting the below error while trying to activate the contract in SAP CC through SOAP UI:

"InvalidTaxException : Bad tax framework feature"

Following configs are there in CC:

  • We have tax disabled in SAP CC.
  • The tax determination type in output is “00”

Please provide any resolution to this error since we are not sure if there are any missing configurations in subscriber account w.r.t tax in S4 system.

Thanks

Abhishek

entry.title: Re: Development Objects are not permitted in ABAP Environ...
entry.content:
Hello, you can use class xco_cp_xlsx to convert excel-string into internal table in cloud development. Try this code:

DATA(lo_xlsx) = xco_cp_xlsx=>document->for_file_content( iv_file_content = iv_xstring )->read_access( ).
DATA(lo_worksheet) = lo_xlsx->get_workbook( )->worksheet->for_name( iv_name = 'Sheet1' ).

DATA(lo_selection_pattern) = xco_cp_xlsx_selection=>pattern_builder->simple_from_to( )->get_pattern( ).

lo_worksheet->select( lo_selection_pattern
)->row_stream(
)->operation->write_to( REF #( rt_records )
)->if_xco_xlsx_ra_operation~execute( ).

Where iv_xstring is an excel file converted into string and rt_records is a DDIC-table type which represents structure of your excel table.

entry.title: Re: Error message F5A 192 when post document for TH
entry.content: Since this issue is related to Finance, not sure, whether you discussed with your FI consultant.  If you are sure, that system should not trigger this error message, you can deactivate via oba5 where, input F5A against Application Area and execute.  There for message number 192, check whether E is set which can be removed.  Again, ensure, you take the concurrence from Business Finance before carrying out this change.

entry.title: Excel Upload By RAP Without Extending on SAP BTP/Business Application Studio Platform?
entry.content:
I want to Upload Data by Excel using RAP Applications, without Extending this Service on SAP BTP Platform Bcoz in my case we dont have any BTP Subscription.

I Already go through with this Blog and it is working fine but here we need to extend the service on BTP Platform. 

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-members/excel-upload-using-rap-part-1/ba-p/13545399

So please suggest how we can acheive the same using RAP Application Only.

entry.title: Re: Problem with a condition appearing twice in the price...
entry.content: An access sequence was not used for the condition. The condition must be determined when the supplier's country is from the EEC and the destination country of the merchandise is Spain. That is why it was done with a calculation formula. I think the problem is due to the fact that when the condition was set, for a couple of months it did not perform the calculation correctly. I think that now when an order is saved when determining the price, since the info record does not have a price indicated, it looks for the price in the last purchase order recorded in the info record, and this turns out to be an order that was recorded incorrectly, and It seems that both the PB00 and the ZESP condition of said order are brought. Is this how pricing works when the last order information is brought in? Are all its conditions brought from that last order? I thought that only the PB00 condition was brought.

entry.title: Re: March Developer Challenge - CloudEvents: Week 2
entry.content:
from cloudevents.conversion import to_structured
from cloudevents.http import CloudEvent
import json

attributes = {
    "type": "com.servicechannel.WorkOrder.Created.v1",
    "source": "https://test.servicechannel.com/workorders",
}
data = {
        "purchaseNumber": "84409626",
        "status": {
            "primary": "open"
        }
    }
event = CloudEvent(attributes, data)

headers, body = to_structured(event)
print(f"Content-Type: {headers['content-type']}\n")
json_body = json.loads(body)
print(f"Sample Cloud Event: \n{json.dumps(json_body, indent=2)}")

entry.title: Re: SAP Analytics Cloud - Activate / Deactivate
entry.content: +1 voice for SCIM API support for user activation/deactivation as soon as possible

entry.title: Re: ABAP RAP - UI refresh after static action call
entry.content:
Hi I have a similar scenario - I would like to have a static factory action to create multiple instances and simply refresh the List Report list.

If I have cardinality [1] it tries to display object page but is empty. when I change to [1..*] no object page which is what is required but list does not refresh.

s/4 Hana on premice 2020 SP02.

@andrefischer - any suggestions? I thought perhaps a Side Effect annotation but guided development doesn't help

Many thanks

Mark

entry.title: Re: SAP CRYSTAL REPORT 2020 support ODBC DRIVER 18 FOR SQ...
entry.content: We observe the same issue when switching from ODBC Driver 17 to OBDC Driver 18 with SQL Server 16.0.1110.1 and 15.0.4355.3. With the reference from https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-q-a/existing-crystal-report-update-data-source-to-odbc-18-driver/qaq-p/12818812, in my opinion, the ODBC function 'SQLProcedures' is suspected of having been changed by Microsoft. However, I could not confirm this with a test of the two driver versions. Both the ODBC driver 17 and the ODBC driver 18 return no result when calling 'SQLProcedures' with the value '<procedure_name>;1'. SQL Profiler, however, shows that the CR Engine does make a second attempt with the pure name. But unfortunately that doesn't change the fact that CR Engine doesn't seem to find the procedure. Hope this helps somehow.

Error in execution of query: invalid table name: Could not find table/view ********::*******_Reuse in schema SAPHANADB: line 1 col 99 (at pos 98)

Appreciate your inputs.

entry.title: SAP TDF Messaging Framework: Event Flow Execution - Processing Sequence Parallelization
entry.content:
Se você gostaria de ler esse blog post em inglês, veja (link).

Hello, everybody, 

This is the first post of a two-post series about the Messaging Framework Flow Execution (/TMF/MF_FLOW_EXEC) solution and its updates provided in SAP Note 3430381.

The Flow Execution (/TMF/MF_FLOW_EXEC) solution is part of the Messaging Framework in the SAP Tax Declaration Framework (SAP TDF) and now enables the parallel execution of event flows in two complementary ways: 
 
1. Parallelization of the processing sequence, covered in this post. 
2. Parallelization of the step processing, covered in the second post of the series. 
 
The main goals of these are optimizing event processing and improving performance in high-volume scenarios. 

What is a processing sequence? 

 To better understand this, we need an example, so imagine a scenario where: 

  1.  1000 events are to be processed  
  2. /TMF/MF_FLOW_EXEC has been scheduled to run a single job 
  3. On the selection screen, only the Flow parameter equals EVENTR and BATCHR. 

This scenario is more suitable for low-volume event scenarios, and the process runs sequentially from start to finish. For example, using the scenario created above, the Release step will only be initialized after all 1000 events have finalized the Validation step.   

 The image above illustrates the processing sequence. 

Optimization in the Processing Sequence  

The Flow Execution (/TMF/MF_FLOW_EXEC) solution is suitable for a high volume of events since it can manage the competition between the processing steps, enabling parallel execution. 

For this to be possible, you must create multiple jobs informing only the steps where you want parallelism or leave them blank. If you leave them blank, the program manages the processing steps and it’s important to use the Endless Execution option. 

To use the Endless Execution option, follow the steps described in Setting up Endless Execution | SAP Help Portal 

To better understand how this optimization works, let's look at an example:  

Assuming there are 10000 events to be processed and 5 jobs have been initialized. When the first job initializes it will reserve the Validation step; the second job will reserve the next step, in this case, the Release step, and so on.  

Therefore, at each step that the event finalizes, this event is immediately available to be processed by the following steps. Because of this, the system does not need to wait for all 10000 events to process to start the next step. 

For more product documentation related to the Messaging Framework - Flow Execution solution access the Help Portal. 

We'd love to hear from you. Leave your comment below if you have any questions or suggestions for an upcoming post. And don't forget to follow the tag SAP Tax Declaration Framework for Brazil here in SAP Community to stay connected with the latest news about the Tax Declaration Framework (TDF). 

See you next time! 

Bruno Maciel 

SAP TDF Development Team

entry.title: Re: ​How to configure Server Sent Events in the SAP Application Router?
entry.content:
Hello Ingmar,

I am trying to implement SSE with approuter as well, and I am also not getting responses back on the event stream as the orginal poster describes. See also my post 
https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-q-a/using-sap-approuter-with-server-sent-events/qaq-p/13634472

However, what you are saying doesn't seem to be true. If I look at the incoming and outgoing network traffic of approuter, it does forward Connection and Accept headers. Our backend behind approuter also maintains and open stream and successfully sends the events on the open event-stream, and they are send from backend to approuter. Only approuter then swallows the events and doesn't forward them to the client. 

So so far no success with SSE, but the problem seems to be within approuter and not with not forwarding the stream request to the backend. 

Best regards,

Manuel

entry.title: Tables on Cloud
entry.content:
Hi Fellow SAP Practitioners,

I am curious to know, how we can get tables, data storage views in cloud?

entry.title: Division by Zero Error
entry.content:
I’m getting a division by zero error with the below formula. Not sure how to edit the formula so it checks for a zero. I have tried the if statements I have found but I keep getting errors. 

 

(Sum ({@Profit},{@Group By} )/Sum ({@Sales w FRT},{@Group By} ))*100

entry.title: SAP TDF – Messaging Framework: Paralelização da Execução do Fluxo de Eventos
entry.content:
If you want to read this blog post in English, see (link).

Olá, pessoal

Este é o primeiro de dois posts em que vou falar sobre a funcionalidade disponível no Messaging Framework do SAP Tax Declaration Framework (SAP TDF), que permite a paralelização da execução do fluxo de eventos (programa /TMF/MF_FLOW_EXEC) de duas maneiras complementares disponibilizadas pela SAP Note (3430381) e que visam agilizar e melhorar a performance do processamento de eventos em cenários de alto volume. O enfoque nesse primeiro post será na sequência de processamento do fluxo de eventos.

Sequência de Processamento

Supondo que existem 1000 eventos a serem processados, que o /TMF/MF_FLOW_EXEC foi agendado para executar um único job e foi informando na tela de seleção apenas o parâmetro “Flow” igual a EVENTR e BATCHR.

Neste cenário, mais indicado para cenários de baixo volume de eventos, o processo será feito de forma sequencial do início ao fim. Ou seja, o passo de “Release” só irá ser iniciado após todos os 1000 eventos terem finalizado o passo de “Validation” e assim por diante.

Otimização na Sequência de Processamento

O programa /TMF/MF_FLOW_EXEC possui a capacidade de gerenciar a concorrência entre os passos de processamento possibilitando a execução de forma paralela, sendo indicado para cenários com alto volume de eventos.

Para isso, é necessário criar múltiplos Jobs informando apenas os passos para os quais deseja o paralelismo ou deixar em branco, ficando a cargo do programa de gerenciar o passo a ser processado.

Neste cenário, onde se busca o paralelismo dos steps, é importante a utilização da opção “Endless”. Essa opção faz com que o job mantenha-se sempre ativo aguardando eventos a serem processados. A opção “End Date for Endless Execution” permite definir uma data limite para que o job fique ativo, podendo ser deixada em branco para que o job seja desativo apenas manualmente.

Supondo que existem 10000 eventos a serem processados e foram inicializados 5 jobs. Quando o primeiro job inicializar ele irá reservar a etapa de “Validation”. Já o segundo job vai selecionar a etapa seguinte, neste caso o passo de “Release”, e assim por diante.

Sendo assim, a cada etapa que o evento finaliza, imediatamente este evento fica disponível para ser processado pelo job do passo seguinte, não mais sendo necessário aguardar o processamento de todos os 1000 eventos para assim iniciar a próxima etapa.

Note que caso ainda não haja eventos prontos para serem processados para alguma das etapas, a opção “Endless” garantirá que o job permaneça ativo, aguardando o tempo informado no campo “Sleep seconds” para tentar processar novamente.

Gostaríamos muito de saber o seu feedback. Deixe seu comentário abaixo caso tenha alguma dúvida ou sugestão para um próximo post. E não esqueça de seguir a tag SAP Tax Declaration Framework for Brazil aqui na SAP Community para ficar ligado nas últimas notícias sobre o Tax Declaration Framework (TDF).

Até a próxima!

Bruno Maciel

Equipe de desenvolvimento do SAP TDF

entry.title: Issue in POC for RA based on Progress Analysis
entry.content:
Hi All,

We are planning to have the RA deriving the POC% from the Progress analysis and which we will maintain in Level 1 WBS. In the back end we have done the settings and also as detailed in the blog Results Analysis Method 7, POC Method Based On Pr... - SAP Community

The following settings are done:

Valuation Method for RA Key with the status is configured in OKG3 with progress version 100 and other settings as below:

VC maintenance as below

Assignment of CE to VC

Question : These cost elements are not existing and was copied from client 000. I hope we should assign an existing one.

SKF 30 defined for POC with usage 3

SKF is assigned to the VC.

However when I run KKA2 I am not getting the POC% which is maintained in the Progress Tab. I am new with this set up but is the POC% taken from the project builder as a part of the standard or is it taken from Progress Analysis after we run CNE1.

Regards,

Suman Misra

entry.title: Re: Builds Failing with new runtime
entry.content:
Hi @radiatorspring, please send us the following information to buildapps@sap.com to further investigate this issue - as it isn't a general one:

- all error messages

- build ID

- tenant ID

Best regards,

Laura (SAP Build Apps Product Management)

entry.title: Re: UKM_BP error R1 206 when BP Role is not the standard UKM000
entry.content:
Hello,
I am an expert in the Business Partner area but not UKM. I think, the UKM application has a check on the role UKM000 rather than the role category UKM000. I suggest you to raise an incident to UKM application at SAP to have a further check.

Regards,

Shwetha

entry.title: How can I send payload as an attachment through ANS Email Alert?
entry.content:
Hello,

Currently we are ANS for exception email alerts, so we have a requirement to send payload as an attachment via ANS email alert with the body what we are currently sending. Can I send attachment ANS? Any suggestions

Thanks in Advance.

Thanks & Regards

Venkat

entry.title: BAdI is not called from Eclipse
entry.content:
Hello,
I'm facing a problem with BAdIs. The requirement is to check package names in different systems. If package name exists in one of these systems, then we should not be able to create package with this name in the other system. This BAdI seems to be working fine from SAP GUI, it stops execution in the above mentioned case. But BAdI is ignored from eclipse and package is created even if it already exists in another system.
Definition name is CTS_CURRENT_PROJECT. How can I make sure that this BAdI works from Eclipse?

entry.title: SAC - Unable to remove signal plus from a number between 0 and 1
entry.content: We want to show minus but not plus in our result area. But the plus is removed from all values except values between 0 and 1

entry.title: Payload Issue in CPI URl
entry.content:
Hello,

I have added the OData adapter to my integration flow. However, when I tested the CPI URL using Postman, it showed a status code of 200 OK but I did not receive any response in the body. It is worth noting that the OData URL contains more than 200,000 records at a time.

entry.title: Re: How can I send payload as an attachment through ANS E...
entry.content:
Hello Venkat! 

Thanks for your interest towards Alert Notification service for SAP BTP (ANS) and raising your question. 

I would like to step in and ideally support a solution. Going now straight to the question - currently there is no such capability in ANS to send attachments over email action type. However, can you please share more details from your specific use case, especially what that attachment would be and why it has to be as an attachment?

I ask these questions as my assumption is that you have this requirements because  you are exceeding the chars limit for the HTML payload template for the ANS email action type? 

I look forward to hearing back from your side.

Kind regards,

Biser

entry.title: Re: CAP with Fiori Elements: Side Effects, Custom Actions, Dynamic Expressions
entry.content: Great blog Ajit! Really helped me in my development!

entry.title: Starting SAP Companion for desktop from Another Server
entry.content:
Hi all,

I'm trying to understand benefits of Starting SAP Companion for desktop from Another Server.

I found this topic ;

https://community.sap.com/t5/human-capital-management-q-a/starting-sap-companion-for-desktop-from-another-server-what-is-a-scenario/qaq-p/12737129#M26404

but I still have  questions.

Prerequisites

  1. SEN Cloud Edition
  2. Apache TomCat

Scenario

  1. I publish SAP Companion as Standard in Producer.
  2. Create *.xdnt file and add it to the SAP Companion
  3. Publish it again with *.xdnt
  4. Upload Published folder to my  web server (Apache)
  5. Create a startling with *.xdnt file
  6. Provide this link to the end users

Result

  1. End users click start link
  2. SAP Companion for desktop will be started
  3. SAP Companion for desktop will be send requests and get responses to my web server

 

Should it work such way?

entry.title: Re: -147 Get CurrentUserInfo failed on SAP connection using DI API
entry.content:
Hi @LuckyLuke ,

I will make a post as soon as we are also able to solve the issue explained. We are currently running SP2305.

Our support team validated all the configuration of SAP, still no issue found. Our next option is to perhaps upgrade client to latest version. 

Kind regards, 
Nicole.

Kind Regards,

Susan

entry.title: SAC - Unable to have [#Year] in measure descriptions
entry.content:
Hi,

we need to have several calculated measures with reference to current year or last year. We need to have [#Year] in the description but this date variable is not available in descriptions. Any way to do this?

entry.title: SAP Build apps upload files intergrate to process automation
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I am trying to integrate files upload from SAP Build Apps into my process automation approval inbox. To achieve this I have followed below steps.

Step1: created a process automation creating input variable type as upload file as shown below :

Step2 :Created a project in SAP Build Apps for upload document as shown below

Step3: Map the variables at data level in SAP Build Apps

When I execute the build apps Form document is upload successfully I am getting toast message as below 

But the issue is unloaded document is not coming to inbox of my process automation, Please advice any suggestion to resolve this issue.

Thanks,

Rupesh

entry.title: Introducing UI5 linter
entry.content:
A linter is a static code analysis tool used to find programming bugs, stylistic errors and suspicious constructs. The usage of a linter has many advantages such as

  • introducing an agreed level of code quality resulting in fewer errors in production,
  • delivering readable and maintainable code,
  • using a consistent code style making code reviews more efficient,
  • validating code security, and
  • ensuring a solid knowledge about code quality for all developers.

The UI5 team introduces UI5 linter to help improve your UI5 code.

A focus of the first version released now are capabilities to validate the compliance to Best Practices for UI5 Developers. Following these best practices for new as well as existing apps has always been … well, … best practice. Going forward, this will be even more relevant as these best practices will be the basis for bringing an app to UI5 2.x. Please refer to Introducing OpenUI5 2.x to understand the approach of removing everything not required to comply with the best practices.

Features

UI5 linter is a static code analysis tool for UI5 projects. It checks JavaScript, XML, JSON, and other files in your project and reports the findings. It lists problematic code, which is not following the UI5 best practices, including

  • usage of deprecated APIs / libraries, which are going to be removed
  • synchronous loading mechanism negatively affecting overall performance, and
  • usage of global variables.

Please note: While the initial version of UI5 linter already provides good value, it is not yet covering all aspects and not all best practices for UI5 2.x. The UI5 team plans more versions in the next weeks, which can reveal more findings.

How To

UI5 linter is installed via the npm package manager and can be used via the CLI. Find details on how to install and use it on https://github.com/SAP/ui5-linter.

Become a UI5 linter hero

  1. Use the new UI5 linter today to
    • improve the static code quality of your UI5 code,
    • validate its compliance to UI5 best practices, and
    • prepare your project for UI5 2.x.

  2. Watch out for upcoming versions of the UI5 linter.

entry.title: Re: FMS not working correctly
entry.content: Thanks a lot!

entry.title: Re: Catalog Features in SAP Datasphere
entry.content:
Hi Fatima,

I have the catalogue user role in my Datasphere tenant but cannot see any assets from the Data Builder or linked SAC.  Any idea why?

Thanks,

Klorn

entry.title: EXIT_SAPLKEDRCOPA_001 not updating the values.
entry.content:
Hi friends,

We have updated the field WW012 in the user exit EXIT_SAPLKEDRCOPA_001. The value is displayed in transaction code KEDR after executing the user exit, but it is not displayed in KE24N.

Thanks and regards,

Vallamuthu M

entry.title: Where can I start learning SAP Activate - Agile Project Delivery?
entry.content:
We  recommend to access our Discovering SAP Activate - Agile Project Delivery learning journey 

Overview
This learning journey will enable you to use agile techniques in SAP projects.

Learning objectives
After completing this learning journey, you will be able to apply the concepts of Agile and Scrum in SAP projects. You'll be able to set up an Agile project and scale agile concepts to larger projects. You'll learn about backlog management and using iterative build for release planning, sprint cycle, and test activities involved in an Agile project.

Prerequisites
Discovering SAP Activate - Implementation Tools and Methodology  

Please ask a question related to the digital learning Journey in the Q&A area

Our SAP Learning Experts will get back to you as soon as possible! We are here to support you.

We appreciate your feedback and we will make sure to continue sharing interesting topics.

entry.title: Product Allocation tables
entry.content:
Hi All, 

Please help me to get the database table details for the Product-to-Product allocation assignment for PAL, the material-plant combination with the PAL object. 

I need those details to develop a PAL custom logic. Thanks in advance.

entry.title: Re: Introducing UI5 linter
entry.content: Interesting. Can we use some kind of ignore file? Currently it checks folders that aren't necessary.

entry.title: Re: Issue in POC for RA based on Progress Analysis
entry.content: I also found that after running the CNE1 only the Non aggregated POC for SKF 10 are filled from the project while the Aggregated POC for SKF 20 is not filled. Just thinking this may be one of the reason why it is also not shown in KKA2

entry.title: Re: Material XXXX status obsolete does not allow the plan...
entry.content:
Hello

Too many materials with a status that forbids the MRP run or with a deletion flag may cause performance issues in MRP. The best practice is to set MRP type ND whenever you set the material status or the deletion flag, so that the planning file entry is deleted and this material is no longer planned by MRP.

A long time ago I wrote this blog, that explains this scenario:

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-sap/performance-improvement-in-mrp-excluding-deleted-materials-from-the/ba-p/13345622#:~:text=If%20you%20have%20too%20many,that%20forbid%20the%20MRP%20run.

Best regards,
Caetano
